---
title: "Quarto Basics"
format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
jupyter: python3
---

[Link to the Quarto markdown code that generated this page](https://github.com/GaryNapier/city_college/blob/main/docs/word_processing/hello_quarto/hello_quarto.qmd){target="_blank"}

# Header 1

Some writing here... Lorem ipsum dolor sit amet, consectetur adipiscing elit...

For markdown basics see: [Markdown Basics](https://quarto.org/docs/authoring/markdown-basics.html){target="_blank"}. 

See also: [Extended syntax](https://www.markdownguide.org/extended-syntax/){target="_blank"}

Paste some of the example code in that page into this file and render after each paste, play around with it. 

## Code blocks:

The result of `1+1` is:

```{python}
print(1+1)
# Comments look like this in the Python code blocks
```

## Inline code: 

The result of `1+1` is `{python} 1+1`

Even better - set the variable in a code block and call the variable inline. You don't really want any code inline, just variables:

```{python}
#| echo: False
# Define 1+1
one_plus_one = 1+1
```

The result of `1+1` is `{python} one_plus_one`

We don't want to include the code block above in the final render, so we used the line
 `#| echo: False` 
 to suppress it. 

There are lots of tricks and options with code blocks and inline code, including sourcing external code scripts, but for now in this file we'll stick to absolute basics. (See [Execution options](https://quarto.org/docs/computations/execution-options.html]){target="_blank"})

**Tips and notes**:

- How to comment (shortcut = ctrl + /)

<!-- Comments look like this in the markdown/html parts -->
`<!-- Comments look like this in the markdown/html parts -->`

- Source: https://quarto.org/docs/get-started/hello/vscode.html

- To render and preview, do one of:
  - execute the `quarto preview <filename>` or `quarto render <filename>` command in the terminal. 
  - use the Ctrl+Shift+K keyboard shortcut, 
  - Preview button (Preview icon) at the top right of the editor

- Render/preview often in case there are any errors. It's easier to fix errors one-by-one rather than writing a huge amount and then having to go through lots of different errors. 

- Further notes: [Python4DS](https://aeturrell.github.io/python4DS/quarto.html){target="_blank"}
